reviews.review_msg: This class seemed easy and fun at first! but when it came to the times that we had to do the assigmnets, there was not a clear rubric in place because she says "I don't want to out you guys in the box, and you guys have to be creative", but the comment she provides is generic and not helpful and she gives the low grades back a month later
Easy course and material, but grades tough. She is not specific enough in the criteria for assignments. Avoid this prof.

Has cancelled more lectures than taught, given us ridiculous "assignments" in lieu: Watch an Amy Schumer movie. Post 250 words about Valentine's Day. Create a playlist. Hasn't marked the one assignment she has given us, and it was due a month and a half ago. Rude and demeaning. I don't know why she's teaching, she clearly has no interest in it.
Lectures were super boring - just read off a script. Tough grader and not very clear with what is required

She is a tough grader. If you speak up in class you will probably do better in the class. The assignments are manageable and she gives good feedback on how to improve. If you listen to the feedback you will be better each time. If you are not willing to work you will not be given a good grade. That only makes sense.

This class was required for my program. The class time was used to discuss our understanding of the readings and she wrote down your name when you said something even if it wasn't exactly related. I had to miss class to go to the doctor and she was not very understanding. You should definitely try to attend all classes or your grade will drop a lot

A personal issue arose for her mid-semester, so our only interactions were her pre-recorded lectures, but she seemed pretty chill. Her lectures aren't super connected to the readings, but if you have any basic knowledge of intersectional feminism, this class is a breeze. 1 midterm 1 final 3 in-class debates & tutorials account for your whole grade
She came off as kinda condescending in our email exchanges. I felt like the grading was lazy and lacked adequate feedback.

Natasha is a very tough grader but I think her expectations are pretty clear in the syllabus. Her readings and lectures are interesting and discussion are very open. To everyone saying she shouldn't read lectures off paper - I have many professors that do this and I don't think it takes anything away from the lecture.
